Love and revenge share the same pulse in a dangerously open threat.
A feared reputation shadows intimacy before it can settle.
Memory fragments a relationship until even the names feel unstable.
Private grief is interrupted by the world's appetite for an explanation.
Romance feels playful, adolescent, and gloriously uncomplicated.
Fantasy becomes a refuge from a reality the narrator cannot stand.
Old cruelty is reframed as fuel for survival and success.
Longing becomes voyeuristic in the aftermath of separation.
The narrator pleads against a fate that keeps withholding love.
Warning the truth becomes another way of being dismissed.
The memory of unfinished love remains suspended between tenderness and delay.
Leaving becomes instinct whenever closeness threatens to harden into capture.
Innocence is guarded with unusual softness against the weight of the world.
Pain is finally placed into narrative form instead of relived in real time.
